title: "The Lottery"
description: "The Lottery" is a short story by Shirley Jackson. Banned in South Africa during Apartheid.
descriptionParagraphs: "The Lottery" is a short story by Shirley Jackson. Banned in South Africa during Apartheid.

Its significance comes from how questions of race, empire, or hierarchy remain live enough to provoke official suppression. As a short story, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in South Africa. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Banned in South Africa during Apartheid. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

banningParagraphs: "The Lottery" entered censorship debates as a short story associated with history, political memory, and race. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political sensitivity and racial politics.
